The '''Ecclessis Figg Room''' was a [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] chamber in the elaborate complex of the [[Trest]] casino in [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]],<ref name="ll" /> the floating [[City/Legends|settlement]] above the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. It was named after [[Ecclessis Figg/Legends|Ecclessis Figg]], founder of the [[City/Legends|city]].<ref name="gg2">''[[Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin]]''</ref><ref name="gg2-2nd">[[Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin'', Second Edition]]</ref> The room contained a bar, a table suited for card-playing and a gallery for spectators. One of the room's door led to an open balcony which offered the panorama of the city.<ref name="ll" />

[[1 ABY/Legends|A year after]] the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] [[Dominic Raynor]] invited the professional gambler [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] to the room for a game of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]]. [[Lobot/Legends|Lobot]], the city's [[Computer/Legends|computer]] liaison officer escorted Calrissian to the room, where the gamblers played two matches. In the first match, the Baron Administrator won the pot, including Calrissian's [[Mu-2 long range shuttle|''Mu-class'' shuttle]], the ''[[Cobra]]''. However, as Lobot revealed, the party was rigged by the dealer, who was hired by the businessman [[Barpotomous Drebble/Legends|Barpotomous Drebble]], an enemy of Calrissian. The gambler didn't had any [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] to continue the play, but Lobot handed him a large sum, the anonymous gift of the fifty thousand workers of the city. In the second match, Calrissian won risked everything and eventually won the entire city from Raynor. After the match and the change of the city's leadership, Lobot revealed to the new Baron Administrator that he rigged the second match to give the city into the hands of a less corrupt man, Lando Calrissian.<ref name="ll">{{Tales|3|Lady Luck (comic story)}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Ecclessis Figg Room's first and only appearance is in the "[[Lady Luck (comic story)|Lady Luck]]" [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[Rich Handley]] and [[Darko Macan]] and published in the [[Star Wars Tales 3|''Star Wars Tales'' 3]] by the [[Dark Horse Comics]] in [[2000]].
